requirements: Define request/response DTOs with explicit fields and types (no leaking ORM models).
Validate every input at the boundary; reject unknown fields when applicable.
Return a consistent error shape (status, code, message); never raw stack traces.
Apply authentication/authorization checks before any work.
Enforce pagination, sort, and limits on collection endpoints.
Document new routes (OpenAPI, README, or inline) and update the API contract.
Add tests covering happy path, validation failure, and unauthorized access.
security: use parameterized queries; never concatenate user input into SQL/NoSQL
set permissive but explicit CORS (or none) for public APIs
log a request id for traceability without logging PII
performance: no N+1 reads inside the handler; load related entities in batch
stream large responses instead of buffering the entire payload
set sensible timeouts on outbound calls
verify: run lint and unit tests for changed handler/controller files
manual or contract test against the new endpoint shape
